[![Listen to this article](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/thumb/8/87/Gnome-mime-sound-openclipart.svg/20px-Gnome-mime-sound-openclipart.svg.png)](/wiki/File:The_Most_Honourable.ogg "Listen to this article")

From Wikipedia, the free encyclopedia

Form of address

Not to be confused with [The Honourable](/wiki/The_Honourable "The Honourable") or [The Right Honourable](/wiki/The_Right_Honourable "The Right Honourable").

The [honorific](/wiki/Honorific "Honorific") prefix "**The Most Honourable**" is a [form of address](/wiki/Form_of_address "Form of address") that is used in several countries. In the [United Kingdom](/wiki/United_Kingdom "United Kingdom"), it precedes the name of a [marquess](/wiki/Marquess "Marquess") or marchioness.[[1]](#cite_note-1)

## Overview

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=The_Most_Honourable&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: Overview")]

In [Jamaica](/wiki/Jamaica "Jamaica"), [Governors-General of Jamaica](/wiki/Governor-General_of_Jamaica "Governor-General of Jamaica"), as well as their spouses, are entitled to be styled "The Most Honourable" upon receipt of the Jamaican [Order of the Nation](/wiki/Order_of_the_Nation "Order of the Nation").[[2]](#cite_note-awards-2) [Prime Ministers of Jamaica](/wiki/Prime_Minister_of_Jamaica "Prime Minister of Jamaica"), and their spouses, are also styled this way upon receipt of the Order of the Nation, which is only given to Jamaican Governors-General and Prime Ministers.[[2]](#cite_note-awards-2)

In [The Bahamas](/wiki/The_Bahamas "The Bahamas"), the style "The Most Honourable" is given to recipients of the Bahamian [Order of the Nation](/wiki/Order_of_the_Nation_(Bahamas) "Order of the Nation (Bahamas)").[[3]](#cite_note-Thompson2018-3)

In [Barbados](/wiki/Barbados "Barbados"), recipients of the [Order of Freedom of Barbados](/wiki/Order_of_Freedom_of_Barbados "Order of Freedom of Barbados") receive the style "The Most Honourable".[[4]](#cite_note-4)

In [Malaysia](/wiki/Malaysia "Malaysia"), the [Prime Minister of Malaysia](/wiki/Prime_Minister_of_Malaysia "Prime Minister of Malaysia") and the Chief Ministers of various Malaysian states are accorded the title *Yang Amat Berhormat* (literally "The Most Honourable" in Malay).[*[citation needed](/wiki/Wikipedia:Citation_needed "Wikipedia:Citation needed")*]

In addition, the names of some groups use this prefix, such as "[His Majesty's Most Honourable Privy Council](/wiki/His_Majesty%27s_Most_Honourable_Privy_Council "His Majesty's Most Honourable Privy Council")" in the United Kingdom.
